title: Vochya ferruginea

Within the kingdom Plantae, Vochya ferruginea belongs to the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Ebenales, and the family Ebenaceae. intro:
There is no information available on a plant called "Vochya ferruginea". However, I can provide information on a similar-sounding plant, "Vochysia ferruginea", which is a species of flowering plant in the family Vochysiaceae.

Vochysia ferruginea is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, characterized by its red-brown bark and yellow flowers.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties.

The bioactive constituents of Vochysia ferruginea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Vochysia ferruginea include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions.
